records.ety: [Prob. from AS. in in + getan to pour: cf. F. linglot, LL. lingotus a mass of gold or silver, extended in the manner of a tongue, and G. einguss, LG. & OE. ingot ingot, a mold for casting metals in. See Found to cast, and cf. Linget, Lingot, Nugget.]

defs: That in which metal is cast; a mold.
quotes: And from the fire he took up his matter And in the ingot put it with merry cheer.

defs: A bar or wedge of steel, gold, or other malleable metal, cast in a mold; a mass of unwrought cast metal.
